jokes.text: Everyone ignores Ben's proud presentation of the website features to focus entirely on a panda mascot playing ping-pong
jokes.setup: Ben excitedly presents important website features like paying tickets and business licenses
jokes.punchline: The entire office gets distracted by a cartoon panda game instead
jokes.explanation: Classic Parks & Rec moment showing how the childlike office ignores important work for silly distractions. Well-structured with clear contrast between Ben's expectations and reality.

jokes.text: This is truly a fine panda game you made. Well done.
jokes.setup: Ben worked hard on a professional government website
jokes.punchline: Ron congratulates him specifically and only on the cartoon panda game
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron Swanson delivery - his deadpan appreciation for something completely frivolous while ignoring the actual accomplishment. Great character integration.

jokes.text: You look like Meryl Streep at the end of Ironweed.
jokes.setup: Andy looks disheveled after a rough day
jokes.punchline: April makes an obscure reference to Meryl Streep looking terrible in a depressing movie
jokes.explanation: Classic April - an unexpectedly specific and dark cultural reference delivered with perfect deadpan timing. Very character-specific.

jokes.text: You wish.
jokes.setup: April insults Andy by comparing him to Meryl Streep looking terrible
jokes.punchline: Andy takes it as a compliment he doesn't deserve
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy response - completely missing that it was an insult and treating it as an aspirational comparison. Great economy.

jokes.text: All these strangers' insurance information
jokes.setup: April mentions getting in four car accidents
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of collecting insurance info like it's a normal outcome
jokes.explanation: Dark humor delivered in April's characteristic deadpan style, treating chaos as mundane.

jokes.text: Ooh, I get it, you have car insurance. Why don't you brag about it?
jokes.setup: Andy mentions having insurance information
jokes.punchline: April sarcastically treats basic responsibility as bragging
jokes.explanation: Classic April sarcasm - her ability to make normal adult behavior sound pretentious is perfect character work.

jokes.text: Ooh, I get it, you have car insurance. Why don't you brag about it?
jokes.setup: Andy mentions getting strangers' insurance information from car accidents
jokes.punchline: April sarcastically treats having basic car insurance as something to brag about
jokes.explanation: Classic April sarcasm - turning a mundane adult responsibility into something mockworthy. Good character voice.

jokes.text: But I've never been because it's mostly middle-aged women and I'm allergic to turkey neck.
jokes.setup: Discussion of bars to check out for bands
jokes.punchline: April claims to be allergic to turkey neck (loose neck skin on older women)
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully specific and absurd April logic - treating an aesthetic preference as a medical condition. Very quotable.

jokes.text: Really? That's the best part. We should split a turkey sometime.
jokes.setup: April mentions being allergic to turkey neck
jokes.punchline: Andy completely misunderstands and thinks she's talking about actual turkey meat
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy obliviousness - completely missing April's mean-spirited observation and turning it into an innocent food suggestion.

jokes.text: This is like the parks equivalent of Bruce Springsteen pulling Courtney Cox onstage. I mean, one minute you're just a regular girl in the crowd, and the next minute you're dancing 10 feet away from freakin' Max Weinberg!
jokes.setup: Leslie gets offered a big job opportunity
jokes.punchline: She makes an overly elaborate comparison to a very specific Springsteen music video moment
jokes.explanation: Very Leslie - turning everything into an overly specific pop culture reference. The specificity of Max Weinberg is perfectly in character but a bit wordy.

jokes.text: I hardly ever do it in my bed on a Saturday night while listening to old spice girls CDs.
jokes.setup: Leslie claims she hates paperwork
jokes.punchline: She accidentally reveals she does exactly that in an overly specific way
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie moment - trying to lie but being too specific and honest. The Spice Girls detail makes it wonderfully character-specific.

jokes.text: I'm kind of like a skinny, handsome, Indian Mario Batali... who doesn't know how to cook.
jokes.setup: Tom compares himself to famous chef Mario Batali
jokes.punchline: He admits he doesn't know how to cook, undermining the entire comparison
jokes.explanation: Classic Tom - grandiose self-comparison that he immediately undercuts with a crucial flaw. Perfect structure and character voice.

jokes.text: Tom, that's so sweet. Thanks. Ow! Oh, my gosh! I didn't even do anything. The voodoo doll is reading my thoughts.
jokes.setup: Tom gives Jerry a voodoo doll of himself
jokes.punchline: Jerry immediately gets hurt and believes the doll is actually working based on his thoughts
jokes.explanation: Classic Jerry physical comedy combined with his gullible nature. The 'reading my thoughts' detail elevates it beyond simple slapstick.

jokes.text: No, we're not gonna do that. We're gonna sit forward and take it hard.
jokes.setup: Ben suggests they can sit back and take it easy with the automated website
jokes.punchline: Leslie's aggressive correction creates an unintentionally sexual-sounding phrase
jokes.explanation: Leslie's intense work ethic leads to an accidentally suggestive phrase. Good economy and character consistency, though slightly cheap humor.

jokes.text: There is New York, the city that never sleeps. And then there is Glenwater, Florida, home of America's most violent Walmart parking lot.
jokes.setup: Contrasting famous city slogans
jokes.punchline: Juxtaposing iconic NYC slogan with absurdly specific negative slogan
jokes.explanation: Excellent contrast joke with great specificity. The Walmart parking lot detail makes it memorably absurd.

jokes.text: So this was one of Eagleton's top sushi restaurants. But you can't out-run the whale meat police forever.
jokes.setup: Showing Tom a former restaurant space
jokes.punchline: Casually reveals it was shut down for serving whale meat
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly unexpected and dark. The phrase 'whale meat police' is absurd and memorable. Delivered with perfect casualness.

jokes.text: Leslie, I told you, I'm not gonna participate in your weird Julia Roberts, cameraman husband fantasy.
jokes.setup: Leslie treats the documentary camera operator as her scene partner
jokes.punchline: Ben reveals this is part of Leslie's specific romantic fantasy about Julia Roberts
jokes.explanation: Great meta joke that acknowledges the documentary format while revealing Leslie's specific and weird romantic fantasies. Very quotable.

jokes.text: Well, the story of my interest level is: It's medium.
jokes.setup: Leslie mentions 'storied past' as a slogan option
jokes.punchline: Perd awkwardly forces the word 'story' into his response about his interest level
jokes.explanation: Perfect Perd Hapley moment - his strange way of speaking where he forces word connections that don't quite work. Excellent character integration.

jokes.text: No, it means 'flower.' I think it means 'John.'
jokes.setup: Someone points out Juan is a man's name when Joan wants to rebrand as Juan
jokes.punchline: Joan confidently states it means flower, then immediately contradicts herself
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joan character moment - confidently wrong then immediately contradicting herself. Great economy showing her shallow knowledge.

jokes.text: Follow-up question, Leslie, show us your boobs!
jokes.setup: Crazy Ira asks a thoughtful question about city integration
jokes.punchline: The Douche's 'follow-up' is completely inappropriate and juvenile
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency for The Douche but pretty basic shock humor. Works for the cringe factor and character establishment.

jokes.text: A smooth and silky evening to you all. On nights like this, when the cold winds blow, and the air is awash in the swirling eddies of our dreams, come with me and find safe haven...in a warm bathtub full of my jazz.
jokes.setup: Ron Swanson, the gruff government worker, performs as a smooth jazz musician
jokes.punchline: His over-the-top sensual jazz persona is completely opposite his normal personality
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character comedy - the contrast between Ron's normally stoic personality and this sensual jazz persona is perfectly executed. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: The current leader is 'Pawnee, welcome to Douche nation.'
jokes.setup: Ben reports on polling results, Leslie expects her preferred slogan to be winning
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous radio show slogan is actually winning
jokes.explanation: Good structure showing how Leslie's earnest efforts can be undermined by juvenile humor. Sets up the conflict nicely.

jokes.text: If I were a dictator, I would throw the Douche in prison without a trial. I would be a very strong dictator, and you would be my bodyguard, and you would lead my army.
jokes.setup: Leslie explains she's not a dictator
jokes.punchline: She immediately describes in detail exactly how she would be a dictator
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie escalation - she can't help but get specific about her hypothetical dictatorship. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: Backwards hat...shows a real lack of respect for authority. I like that.
jokes.setup: Leslie tries to appear 'cool' for the radio DJs
jokes.punchline: She completely misunderstands what makes someone cool, focusing on authority issues
jokes.explanation: Great Leslie moment showing how her government mindset colors everything, even when trying to be rebellious.

jokes.text: I'm just psyched to be in the studio, you know? I'm just...chillin' in the studes with my dudes.
jokes.setup: Leslie tries to use cool slang to fit in with the radio DJs
jokes.punchline: Her attempt at slang is forced and awkward, especially 'studes'
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy as Leslie tries way too hard. 'Studes' is particularly awkward and memorable.

jokes.text: On a scale, from 1 to ga-ga-ga-going! How would you rate Emma Watson's boobs?
jokes.setup: Setting up a rating system
jokes.punchline: Crude sound effect as the top rating
jokes.explanation: Deliberately crude humor that establishes the radio show's low-brow nature but isn't particularly clever.

jokes.text: First of all, I would rate her acting as an 'A.'
jokes.setup: Radio hosts ask Leslie to rate Emma Watson's boobs
jokes.punchline: Leslie purposely misunderstands and rates her acting instead
jokes.explanation: Smart misdirection that stays true to Leslie's character. She tries to redirect crude humor into something more appropriate.

jokes.text: More like double D minus.
jokes.setup: Leslie gives an 'A minus' rating
jokes.punchline: Host turns it into crude bra size reference
jokes.explanation: Predictable crude pun that serves the show's character but isn't particularly clever.

jokes.text: I have a twin brother. What? I have a twin brother. You are Duke Silver? Yes, I am Duke Silver.
jokes.setup: April discovers Ron is Duke Silver
jokes.punchline: Ron's panicked first response is to claim he has a twin brother, which he immediately abandons
jokes.explanation: Great Ron moment showing his panic at being discovered. The immediate abandonment of the twin brother lie is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: If my secret got out, this office would be waist-deep in women's undergarments.
jokes.setup: Ron explains why he keeps Duke Silver secret
jokes.punchline: He claims women would literally throw underwear at the office
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron confidence about his sexual magnetism as Duke Silver. The specific image is both absurd and believable coming from him.

jokes.text: This is my mom's dumper! Oh, mommy! I don't know how you got your hands on that. I took it.
jokes.setup: Radio show game about rating body parts
jokes.punchline: The Douche reveals he has and took inappropriate photos of his own mother
jokes.explanation: Deliberately gross and uncomfortable, which fits The Douche character but relies heavily on shock value rather than clever writing.

jokes.text: Or you know what would also be kind of dope for the people of Douche nation to do? It's vote for another slogan. Like, you know, 'Storied past, bright future'? But...I don't care. I don't give a fart, bros.
jokes.setup: Leslie tries to manipulate the radio audience while pretending to be cool
jokes.punchline: Her forced slang and transparent agenda are painfully obvious
jokes.explanation: Good cringe as Leslie's manipulation attempt is transparent. 'I don't give a fart' is particularly awkward trying-to-be-cool language.

jokes.text: Lesbo Baggins, you know where my stick is? You... Oh! Found my stick. Oh, where did you find it? Up Leslie Knope's butt!
jokes.setup: Crazy Ira looks for his stick and asks Leslie
jokes.punchline: The Douche delivers juvenile insult about finding it up Leslie's butt
jokes.explanation: Very juvenile humor that fits the characters but isn't particularly clever. Sets up the running gag that becomes the leading slogan.

jokes.text: I am ending my secret musical career. I have to kill Duke Silver. Sorry, Andrew. I would rather never play again than have everyone know my secret. Good-bye, Duke Silver. May you rest...in jazz.
jokes.setup: Ron decides to quit music to protect his secret
jokes.punchline: His overly dramatic farewell to his saxophone, ending with 'May you rest in jazz'
jokes.explanation: Beautiful character moment combining Ron's stoicism with unexpected sentimentality. 'May you rest in jazz' is perfectly quotable and memorable.

jokes.text: Yeah, yeah. I voted for that one.
jokes.setup: Leslie complaining about offensive slogan leading
jokes.punchline: April casually admits she voted for it
jokes.explanation: Perfect April moment - her deadpan admission and lack of shame is exactly right for her character's antagonistic relationship with Leslie.

jokes.text: Yeah, yeah. I voted for that one.
jokes.setup: Leslie explains the insulting slogan about a stick up her butt is winning
jokes.punchline: April casually admits she voted for it
jokes.explanation: Perfect April moment - maximum cruelty with minimal effort. The deadpan delivery and economy make it brutally funny.

jokes.text: Should take about two minutes. Just PayPal me some bitcoins when you get a chance.
jokes.setup: April offering to help with technical problem
jokes.punchline: Casual request for cryptocurrency payment
jokes.explanation: Good April line showing her tech-savvy millennial side. Bitcoin reference dates the episode but works for the character.

jokes.text: No, another Steven Spielberg-themed restaurant. The German place downtown... Schindler's Lunch.
jokes.setup: Tom asks if Jean-Ralphio opened another dinosaur restaurant
jokes.punchline: He opened a Holocaust-themed restaurant with a terrible pun name
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark and inappropriate in a way that's perfect for Jean-Ralphio. The pun is terrible but memorable. Pushes boundaries effectively.

jokes.text: No can do. That's a load-bearing T-Rex.
jokes.setup: Tom suggests moving the T-Rex decoration
jokes.punchline: Jean-Ralphio claims it's structurally necessary using construction terminology
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist humor combining construction jargon with a ridiculous dinosaur statue. Perfect economy and very quotable.

jokes.text: Look what they did to Peebo. He's wearing a hat made of penises.
jokes.setup: Hackers attacked the website after Leslie's actions
jokes.punchline: They specifically vandalized the innocent panda mascot in a crude way
jokes.explanation: Good visual callback showing consequences of Leslie's actions. Somewhat juvenile but effective escalation of the conflict.

jokes.text: I mean, have I really pulled my last bloated raccoon carcass from a public fountain?
jokes.setup: Leslie worries about what she'll miss in a management role
jokes.punchline: She's nostalgic about grotesque tasks like removing dead animals
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie character moment - finding sentiment in disgusting government work. The specificity makes it both gross and touching.

jokes.text: I somehow ran my belt through a slat in this chair so I'm kind of...I'm kind of stuck to it now.
jokes.setup: Leslie calls Jerry into the meeting
jokes.punchline: He's physically trapped by his own incompetence with basic clothing
jokes.explanation: Classic Jerry physical comedy. The specific nature of the mishap (belt through chair slat) elevates it beyond simple clumsiness.

jokes.text: When they say 2% milk, I don't know what the other 98% is.
jokes.setup: Andy sharing more secrets
jokes.punchline: Fundamental misunderstanding of how percentages work
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Andy logic showing his complete misunderstanding of basic concepts delivered with innocent confusion.

jokes.text: When they say 2% milk, I don't know what the other 98% is.
jokes.setup: Andy shares his secrets with Ron
jokes.punchline: He fundamentally misunderstands how percentages work with milk fat content
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy logic - taking a completely wrong approach to understanding something basic. Very quotable and relatable confusion.

jokes.text: I once threw beer at a swan, and then it attacked my niece Rebecca.
jokes.setup: Andy shares increasingly ridiculous secrets
jokes.punchline: An innocent-sounding action led to his niece being attacked by wildlife
jokes.explanation: Great escalation with perfect Andy logic - the action and consequence are both absurd but somehow make sense for him.

jokes.text: When I was a baby, my head was so big scientists did experiments on me.
jokes.setup: Andy's secret sharing continues
jokes.punchline: Claims childhood scientific experimentation
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist Andy moment - the casual mention of being experimented on as a baby is wonderfully weird.

jokes.text: Cologne for watches.
jokes.setup: April giving Tom 'watch cologne' to cheer him up
jokes.punchline: Completely nonsensical product concept delivered seriously
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd April gift that makes no sense but is delivered with complete seriousness.

jokes.text: Things used to make me so happy. But I've grown a lot in the last year. Now I just want one big thing, my own restaurant, named after me, that makes me so much money I can buy anything I want.
jokes.setup: Tom talks about growing and maturing
jokes.punchline: His idea of growth is wanting even more materialistic things than before
jokes.explanation: Good Tom character consistency - he thinks he's grown but has just shifted his materialism to a bigger scale. Nice irony.

jokes.text: I think the slogan should be 'Pawnee, home of crackers, the orangest goldfish in Indiana.'
jokes.setup: Public forum for serious slogan suggestions
jokes.punchline: Completely random suggestion about orange goldfish crackers
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor showing how public forums can go wrong. Random but memorable.

jokes.text: No, that's my slogan idea. 'Who even needs a slogan?' And then a big picture of me flipping everybody off.
jokes.setup: Person questions the need for slogans
jokes.punchline: They want their anti-slogan sentiment as the official slogan, with their picture giving the finger
jokes.explanation: Clever meta joke about the process itself, with good escalation to the visual element. Self-aware humor.

jokes.text: Who even needs a slogan? And then a big picture of me flipping everybody off.
jokes.setup: Citizen asking rhetorical question about slogans
jokes.punchline: Reveals it's actually a crude slogan suggestion with visual
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection showing how public forums can go off the rails.

jokes.text: Back off. 'You're' is spelled wrong. It should be 'y-o-u-r.' Oops, I didn't catch it. Thank you. No, no, no, she made a possessive. She's getting further away.
jokes.setup: Citizens try to correct spelling errors on a well-meaning woman's slogan sign
jokes.punchline: They keep getting the corrections wrong themselves, making it worse
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing how helpful intentions can go wrong. Leslie's observation about getting 'further away' is perfect.

jokes.text: You fixed the mistakes. I held it together for the entire meeting. Let me have this one.
jokes.setup: Leslie tries to delegate and not micromanage
jokes.punchline: She immediately fixes the sign after claiming to have shown restraint
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie moment - she tried so hard to delegate but can't help herself when she sees mistakes. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: Then I'm sure you are okay with the fact that they installed the sign the wrong way and it's welcoming you to Pawnee as you're leaving.
jokes.setup: Leslie claims she's done micromanaging
jokes.punchline: Ben reveals another mistake that will definitely trigger her need to fix things
jokes.explanation: Great setup/punchline showing Ben knows exactly how to push Leslie's buttons. The irony of a welcome sign facing the wrong way is perfect.

jokes.text: Leslie, I love you, very much. But this is the stupidest thing you've ever said. You'd be amazing. And everyone's gonna be in awe of how amazing you are, so just shut your month.
jokes.setup: Ben gives Leslie a loving, supportive speech
jokes.punchline: He accidentally says 'shut your month' instead of 'mouth'
jokes.explanation: Sweet moment undercut by a silly verbal slip. The malapropism works because it comes during an emotional moment.
